title: jarava subaristata
intro: Jarava subaristata, also known as Queñua or Quenua, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant's leaves and bark have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Jarava subaristata include the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Jarava subaristata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
